commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Amazon Bedrock prompt-cache eligibility, payload cachePoint injection, docs, and tests so Nova generation models can use Bedrock prompt caching without Claude-only TTL fields.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +88, Docs +1. Total +136 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blocker: source inspection shows the provider wrapper is enabled for Nova while the unchanged shared resolver still drops direct-Nova cacheRetention before the Bedrock stream path. Live AWS Nova behavior was not reproduced in this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer live AWS Nova proof plus a compatibility repair before merge; this is not a safe repair-lane candidate while the real-behavior proof gate remains external.

target.title: feat(bedrock): enable prompt caching for Nova models
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #29707. Amazon Nova generation models on Bedrock were still treated as unsupported non-Anthropic models, so OpenClaw forced `cacheRetention: "none"` and never emitted Bedrock `cachePoint` blocks for Nova requests.

## Why This Change Was Made

AWS documents explicit prompt caching support for Amazon Bedrock models, including Nova generation models: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/prompt-caching.html

This change teaches the shared Bedrock cache policy to recognize Nova Micro, Lite, Pro, Premier, and Nova 2 Lite refs, lets direct Nova requests keep their cache settings, and keeps the existing no-cache fallback for unsupported non-Anthropic Bedrock models. For Nova, `cacheRetention: "long"` emits the default Bedrock cache point without reusing Claude's `ttl: "1h"` field.

## User Impact

Users can enable prompt caching for Bedrock Nova text models without hitting the previous runtime no-cache override. Unsupported non-Anthropic Bedrock models, such as Mistral, continue to be protected from unsupported cache metadata.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/amazon-bedrock/index.test.ts extensions/amazon-bedrock/stream.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`

Live Bedrock Nova request proof was not run because this Codex environment does not have AWS Bedrock credentials; opening as draft for maintainer/live verification.

AI-assisted contribution.
target.author: outdog-hwh
